---
name: clarify-idea
description: |
  Use this skill to produce or evolve `.specs/spec.md`. Two modes
  driven by whether the spec already exists:
  • **Fresh mode** — vague idea, blank page. Drives a 3-round interview
    (problem → users → must-haves → out of scope → success metric).
  • **Update mode** — spec exists, a new requirement / feature request
    / triaged feedback needs folding in. Targeted interview + append-only
    `## Updates` block (never rewrites). Invoked by `feedback-triager`
    after a confirmed feature request, or directly by the maintainer.
---

# Clarify Idea

Stage 1 (and stage 9 update sub-step) of the lifecycle autopilot pipeline.
Every downstream stage (spec-driven dev, E2E, demo, marketing, posts,
monitoring, feedback) depends on a clear spec. This skill produces it
and keeps it up to date.

## Mode detection — first thing this skill does

```
if (.specs/spec.md exists)   → Update mode  (jump to ## Update mode)
else if (legacy spec.md exists at workspace root)
                              → migrate to .specs/spec.md, then Update mode
else                          → Fresh mode  (continue below)
```

Fresh mode = blank-page interview. Update mode = targeted append-only
interview. Same skill, same interview voice, different starting state.

## When to invoke

**Fresh mode** triggers — the user says one of:

- "I have an idea for X — help me scope it"
- "Let's start a new feature: …"
- "Write a spec for …"
- "What's the smallest thing I could ship for …"

Or: the user starts implementing without a spec and the request is
ambiguous (you can't enumerate the acceptance criteria from a single
sentence).

**Update mode** triggers:

- "Update the spec", "add this to v0", "the user asked for X — fold it in"
- `feedback-triager` hands off after a confirmed feature request
- Post-release: capture follow-ups for v0.1 / v1

## Output

A single file at `<workspace>/.specs/spec.md` with this skeleton:

> **Why `.specs/`?** Every downstream artifact in the lifecycle lives
> under `.specs/` — `lifecycle.md` (orchestrator ledger), `stories.md`
> + `scenarios/` (user-stories skill), `updates/` (this skill, update
> mode), `monitoring/` (post-monitor), `feedback/` (feedback-triager).
> Keep the spec next to its siblings; never write to the workspace
> root. Create the directory if it does not exist (`mkdir -p .specs`).

```markdown
# <Product / Feature name>

## Problem
<One paragraph: who hurts, how often, what happens today>

## Users
<List of user personas + the one primary persona we optimise for>

## Must-haves (v0)
- [ ] <thing>
- [ ] <thing>

## Out of scope (v0)
- <thing we are NOT doing>
- <thing we are NOT doing>

## Success metric
<One measurable signal that tells us v0 worked>

## Open questions
- <thing the user couldn't answer yet>

---
*Spec generated by clarify-idea skill on <date>. Update via the same
skill, do not hand-edit unless trivial.*
```

If `.specs/spec.md` already exists, you're in **Update mode** — see the
dedicated section below. The Fresh-mode interview (Rounds 1-3) only runs
on a blank page.

## Interview flow (Fresh mode)

Drive the interview with the `ask_user` tool. Ask **two to four
questions per turn** (no more — long question batches scare users
away). Use `multiSelect: true` for "pick all that apply" style; default
to single-select otherwise. Always allow `allowFreeformInput: true`
so the user can override your options.

### Round 1 — Problem & persona

```
ask_user({
  questions: [
    {header:"problem", question:"In one sentence, what's the pain you're solving?"},
    {header:"who",     question:"Who feels it the most?",
                       options:[/* 3-5 inferred personas */],
                       multiSelect:true},
    {header:"frequency", question:"How often does this pain show up?",
                       options:["Daily","Weekly","Rarely","Don't know yet"]},
  ]
})
```

### Round 2 — v0 scope

```
ask_user({
  questions: [
    {header:"must-have", question:"Pick the must-haves for the first usable version (v0)",
                         options:[/* 4-8 inferred features */],
                         multiSelect:true},
    {header:"timeline",  question:"How fast does v0 need to ship?",
                         options:["Today","This week","This month","No deadline"]},
  ]
})
```

### Round 3 — Out-of-scope & success

```
ask_user({
  questions: [
    {header:"not-now",  question:"Anything you DON'T want in v0?"},
    {header:"success",  question:"How will you know v0 worked? Pick the strongest signal.",
                        options:[/* 3-4 measurable signals */]},
  ]
})
```

After Round 3, write `.specs/spec.md`. Show it to the user with a one-line
summary and ask `ask_user({question:"Spec looks right? (yes / tweak)"})`.

If they say "tweak", drill into the section they want to change with one
more `ask_user` round, then rewrite that section.

## Update mode

Reached when `.specs/spec.md` already exists. Goal: fold a new
requirement in **without rewriting history**. Append-only.

### 1. Read first

Load `.specs/spec.md` fully. Note current `## Must-haves`, `## Out of
scope`, `## Success metric`, `## Open questions`, and any prior
`## Updates` blocks.

### 2. Capture the trigger

What's prompting the update? An issue body, a user quote, a triage
decision from `feedback-triager`. Save the seed as
`.specs/updates/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<short-slug>.md` (interview log lives here
too).

Extract from the trigger:
- **Asked-for behavior** — verbatim quote in the user's words
- **Inferred class** — bug fix / new must-have / nice-to-have / out-of-scope
- **Affected sections** — which parts of the spec it touches

### 3. Targeted interview (max 3 rounds)

Skip rounds whenever the answer is obvious from the trigger.

```
Round 1 — class
  ask_user({
    question: "Where does this go?",
    options: [
      "Add to ## Must-haves (v0)",
      "Add to ## Out of scope (move to v0.1)",
      "Update ## Success metric",
      "Just a clarification — note in ## Open questions",
      "Reject — doesn't fit the product"
    ]
  })

Round 2 — wording  (only if Round 1 said "add")
  ask_user({
    question: "Phrase the must-have as a single line:",
    options: [
      "<auto-suggest from trigger>",
      "<alt suggestion>",
      "Let me write it"
    ]
  })

Round 3 — gate
  ask_user({
    question: "Apply this update?",
    options: ["Yes, append", "Let me edit first", "Cancel"]
  })
```

### 4. Append, don't rewrite

**Critical:** preserve the original spec verbatim. Add an update block
at the bottom (creating the `## Updates` heading if it's the first
update):

```markdown
---
## Updates

### <YYYY-MM-DD> — <short-slug>
- **Trigger:** <issue #N | user quote | etc.>
- **Class:** <must-have | out-of-scope | clarification>
- **Change:**
  - [ ] <new must-have line>           ← if must-have
  - or: moved "<X>" from out-of-scope to must-haves
  - or: added to Open questions: "<text>"
```

If the change is a **must-have**, also append the unchecked checkbox
to the canonical `## Must-haves (v0)` list (so the orchestrator's
stage-2 implement-loop picks it up).

### 5. Update-mode guardrails

- **Never delete from the original spec.** Strike-through (`~~text~~`)
  if a section becomes obsolete; document why in the update block.
- **Never add ≥ 3 must-haves in one update.** Larger feature requests
  reset to Fresh mode (full re-spec) — archive the old spec to
  `.specs/spec.archive-<date>.md` and start over.
- **Always commit each update separately** (`docs(spec): update —
  <slug>`). Easier to revert if the feature request is later withdrawn.

### 6. Update-mode hand-off

After append + commit:
- New must-have added → ask: "Implement now or later?" If now, hand off
  to the `spec-driven-dev` orchestrator agent at stage 2.
- Only a clarification → stop, report back.

## Heuristics

- **Always pre-populate option lists.** Empty open-ended questions get
  empty answers. Infer 3–5 plausible options from what the user has
  already said, present them as multi-select, leave freeform on as the
  escape hatch.
- **Cap rounds at 3.** If after Round 3 the spec still has 5+ open
  questions, write it anyway — the spec is a living doc.
- **Never ask about implementation details** (framework, DB, tooling).
  That's stage 2 (the `spec-driven-dev` agent). Stay at the *what* / *why* layer.
- **Reuse `manage_todo_list`** to show the user the live list of
  must-haves as they accumulate — they'll spot gaps faster.

## Hand-off

After `.specs/spec.md` is written and confirmed, tell the user:

> "Spec saved to `.specs/spec.md`. Next, ask me to *implement v0* and I'll
> use the spec-driven-dev agent to plan a PR, write tests, and ship
> the first iteration. (Optionally, run the **`user-stories`** skill
> first to expand each `## Must-haves` bullet into formal user stories
> + scenarios at `.specs/stories.md` and `.specs/scenarios/`.)"

Do **not** start implementing yourself in the same turn — let the user
explicitly authorise the move from clarification to coding. This
preserves the contract that stage 1 is cheap and reversible.

## `.specs/` layout (siblings produced by other lifecycle skills)

```
.specs/
├── spec.md                     ← this skill (clarify-idea)
├── lifecycle.md                ← spec-driven-dev orchestrator ledger
├── stories.md                  ← user-stories
├── track.md                    ← user-stories (test status)
├── scenarios/
│   ├── us-NN-*.md              ← user-stories (per story)
│   └── happy-paths.md          ← user-stories (E2E journeys)
├── updates/<date>-<slug>.md    ← clarify-idea, update mode (interview log)
├── feedback/<N>-spec.md        ← feedback-triager (informal bug spec)
└── monitoring/<date>.md        ← post-monitor (digest)
```

Always create `.specs/` if missing (`mkdir -p .specs`). Never write
spec artifacts to the workspace root.

## Related skills / agents

- [`build-ai-app`](../build-ai-app/SKILL.md) — once `.specs/spec.md` exists,
  this skill helps map it to the 5-Layer (UI / orchestrator / sub-agents
  / skills / tools) architecture.
- [`user-stories`](../user-stories/SKILL.md) — turn `## Must-haves`
  bullets into formal user stories + scenarios for stage 4 verification
  (sibling artifacts at `.specs/stories.md`, `.specs/scenarios/`).
- [`spec-driven-dev`](../../agents/spec-driven-dev.agent.md) (agent,
  stage 2) — picks up `.specs/spec.md` and turns it into code.
- [`e2e-as-real-user`](../e2e-as-real-user/SKILL.md) (stage 4) —
  drives the implemented app through the must-haves like a real user.
- [`agent-browser`](../agent-browser/SKILL.md) — low-level web
  driver, used by `e2e-as-real-user`.
